16 HR & Recruiting Voices to Follow on LinkedIn

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/philstrazzulla?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Phil Strazzulla](https://www.linkedin.com/in/philstrazzulla?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

3mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fphilstrazzulla_ive-seen-a-few-posts-like-this-and-wanted-activity-7437477906945077249-pReE\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I've seen a few posts like this and wanted to highlight people I've learned a lot from. Here are 16 HR and recruiting voices worth following on LinkedIn: 1. [Amy Mencarelli, PHR, MBA](https://www.linkedin.com/in/amymencarelli?trk=public_post-text) Shares grounded, operator-level HR leadership insights focused on business impact and org design. Strong at translating HR work into language that executives actually care about. 2. [Tracie Sponenberg](https://www.linkedin.com/in/traciesponenberg?trk=public_post-text) Former CPO at The Granite Group, now fractional executive focused on deskless and frontline workers in manufacturing and distribution. Writes about AI in HR, wellbeing, and what people-first looks like in industries that still run on paper. 3. [Adriano Herdman](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/adrianoherdman1?trk=public_post-text) Writes candid, experience-driven content on recruiting strategy, hiring precision, and TA maturity, especially in startups and high-growth environments. 4. [Michael Brown](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michaelbrown91281?trk=public_post-text) Writes about building AI-native recruiting operations, global hiring at scale, and treating talent acquisition like a product. 5. [Alex Seiler](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexseiler?trk=public_post-text) Fractional CPO who has operated across startups, mid-market, and Fortune 500s. Writes about strategic people leadership, org transformation, and scaling via people. 6. [Ben Eubanks](https://www.linkedin.com/in/beneubanks?trk=public_post-text) Research-led voice on HR technology, learning, and workforce trends. 7. [Keri Ohlrich, PhD](https://www.linkedin.com/in/keriohlrich?trk=public_post-text) Co-founder of Abbracci Group and co-author of The Way of the HR Warrior. Writes about leadership development, culture, and what it means to show up as a real HR practitioner. 8. [Lars Schmidt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/larsschmidt?trk=public_post-text) Advocates for human-centered HR, ethical leadership, and modern people practices. Blends culture, leadership, and HR transformation with a strong values-driven perspective. 9. [Daniel Space](https://www.linkedin.com/in/danfromhr?trk=public_post-text) Breaks down how companies actually make decisions on hiring, pay, and promotions in plain language most HR pros won't say out loud. 10. [Hung Lee](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/hunglee?trk=public_post-text) Acts as a signal filter for recruiters by curating trends, tools, experiments, and labor market shifts. Great follow for staying current without drowning in recruiting noise. 11. [Hailey George, MBA](https://www.linkedin.com/in/haileygeorge?trk=public_post-text) Fractional Head of HR for small businesses and founder-led teams. Writes about practical people systems, compliance, and building HR infrastructure without the enterprise overhead. 12. [Stacy Donovan Zapar](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stacyzapar?trk=public_post-text) Shares practical guidance on how companies attract talent authentically, not just through perks or slogans. 13. [Cammas Freeman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/cammasfreeman?trk=public_post-text) Shares recruiting strategy that blends AI tools with a human-first approach to hard-to-fill roles. 14. [Kieran Snyder](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kieran-snyder?trk=public_post-text) Data-first thinker on AI transformation, performance management, feedback systems, and people analytics. 15. [Kyle Lagunas](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kylelagunas?trk=public_post-text) Primary researcher and analyst focused on talent transformation, technology strategy, and the future of work. Writes about what leading organizations are doing differently, backed by data rather than opinion. 16. [Hannah Morgan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/hannahmorgan?trk=public_post-text) Bridges the gap between recruiters and job seekers with practical advice on hiring, job search strategy, and career navigation rooted in real recruiting experience. Who would you add?

[139](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fphilstrazzulla_ive-seen-a-few-posts-like-this-and-wanted-activity-7437477906945077249-pReE\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [65 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2Fphilstrazzulla_ive-seen-a-few-posts-like-this-and-wanted-activity-7437477906945077249-pReE\&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephaniebyrnessphr?trk=public_post_comment_actor-image)

[Stephanie Byrnes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephaniebyrnessphr?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name) 3mo

[Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fphilstrazzulla_ive-seen-a-few-posts-like-this-and-wanted-activity-7437477906945077249-pReE\&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Phil, love the post, list, and tags!! I’ve followed many of these voices, especially Tracie Sponenberg for her insights on the deskless workforce and she has a critical focus in the Manufacturing and Life Sciences sectors (which is where I have lived for years)... I would add Steve Browne to this list [https://www.linkedin.com/in/steveb5](https://www.linkedin.com/in/steveb5?trk=public_post_comment-text)/ . His HR philosophy is a constant reminder for those of us in the trenches that our work is about people first, even when we’re knee-deep in M\&A or organizational design. I find his energy essential for staying grounded.

[Anessa Fike](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anessafike?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name) 2mo

[Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fphilstrazzulla_ive-seen-a-few-posts-like-this-and-wanted-activity-7437477906945077249-pReE\&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

[Phil Strazzulla](https://www.linkedin.com/in/philstrazzulla?trk=public_post_comment-text) While I love this list, I'd love to see more people from diverse backgrounds on this list as well, like [Tracie Sponenberg](https://www.linkedin.com/in/traciesponenberg?trk=public_post_comment-text) suggested. As I'd recommend a few of those same people! Also would recommend [T. Tara Turk-Haynes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ttaraturkhaynes?trk=public_post_comment-text), Tameka N. Allen, [Torin Ellis](https://www.linkedin.com/in/torinellis?trk=public_post_comment-text), [Rhona Barnett-Pierce](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rhonabarnettpierce?trk=public_post_comment-text), [Ayanna Kelly](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ayannaskelly?trk=public_post_comment-text), [Ayanna E. Jackson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ayannaejacksoncareercoach?trk=public_post_comment-text), [Alana Cheeks-Lomax](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alanac?trk=public_post_comment-text), [Jaclyn Mayes-Avíla, MHRM](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jaclynmayes-av%C3%ADla?trk=public_post_comment-text), and many more!

More Relevant Posts

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/riddhi-thakkar-bb80bb213_hr-is-not-just-about-people-its-about-understanding-activity-7437482545677717504-aP7-)

[](https://in.linkedin.com/in/riddhi-thakkar-bb80bb213?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Riddhi Thakkar](https://in.linkedin.com/in/riddhi-thakkar-bb80bb213?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

3mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Friddhi-thakkar-bb80bb213_hr-is-not-just-about-people-its-about-understanding-activity-7437482545677717504-aP7-\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

HR is not just about people. It’s about understanding people through data. Many times HR is seen as a “people management” function. But great HR leaders don’t rely only on intuition. They rely on metrics that reveal what’s really happening inside the organization. Because numbers tell stories that conversations sometimes miss. For example: 📊 Time to Hire tells us how efficient our hiring process is. 💰 Cost per Hire shows whether our recruitment strategy is sustainable. 🎯 Quality of Hire reflects if we are bringing in the right talent. Then there are deeper signals: • Early Turnover – Are new hires leaving too soon? • Employee Engagement – Are people truly connected to their work? • Absenteeism – Is something affecting motivation or well-being? • Revenue per Employee – How effectively is talent contributing to growth? • Leadership Effectiveness – Are managers actually enabling teams? These metrics help HR move from reactive to strategic. Because when HR understands these numbers, we can: ✔ Improve hiring decisions ✔ Build stronger teams ✔ Reduce employee turnover ✔ Create healthier workplace cultures ✔ Align people strategy with business growth At the end of the day, HR metrics are not just numbers on a dashboard. They are signals about how people experience the workplace. And when HR listens to these signals, organizations grow stronger. 💬 I’m curious to know from fellow HR professionals: Which HR metric do you think leaders often ignore but actually matters the most? Let’s learn from each other. If you found this useful, feel free to save or share it with your HR network. HR is not just about people. It’s about understanding people through data. Many times HR is seen as a “people management” function. But great HR leaders don’t rely only on intuition. They rely on metrics that reveal what’s really happening inside the organization. Because numbers tell stories that conversations sometimes miss. For example: 📊 Time to Hire tells us how efficient our hiring process is. 💰 Cost per Hire shows whether our recruitment strategy is sustainable. 🎯 Quality of Hire reflects if we are bringing in the right talent. Then there are deeper signals: • Early Turnover – Are new hires leaving too soon? • Employee Engagement – Are people truly connected to their work? • Absenteeism – Is something affecting motivation or well-being? • Revenue per Employee – How effectively is talent contributing to growth? • Leadership Effectiveness – Are managers actually enabling teams? These metrics help HR move from reactive to strategic. Because when HR understands these numbers, we can: ✔ Improve hiring decisions ✔ Build stronger teams ✔ Reduce employee turnover ✔ Create healthier workplace cultures ✔ Align people strategy with business growth At the end of the day, HR metrics are not just numbers on a dashboard. They are signals about how people experience the workplace. And when HR listens to these signals, organizations grow stronger.

[19](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Friddhi-thakkar-bb80bb213_hr-is-not-just-about-people-its-about-understanding-activity-7437482545677717504-aP7-\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/fernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti)

[](https://br.linkedin.com/in/fernanda-lavini-ramos?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Fernanda Lavini Ramos](https://br.linkedin.com/in/fernanda-lavini-ramos?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Last week (March 26-27), part of my team and I had the opportunity to attend the StartSe HR Summit. I am generally not a fan of long posts, but these days left me with key insights that are too valuable not to share:   Hybrid Intelligence: HR’s new role is to manage the symbiotic integration of AI’s analytical power with human strategic thinking. The goal is to cut through data overload and focus on true value generation.   Brazil as an Innovation Hub: Our market’s high tech-acceptance rate makes it fertile ground for innovation pilots, accelerating the implementation of cutting-edge HR tools.   The New Talent Landscape: As the market grows more complex, we are transitioning toward flexible, results-oriented models. This brings the rise of on-demand professionals and "intelligent recruitment" focused on cultural alignment and raw potential—all while making retention a much greater challenge.   Leadership as Facilitators: In this environment, leaders must foster a learning culture, act as facilitators of change, and remain the ultimate owners of strategic and ethical decisions.   Intentional Social Impact: There is a growing necessity for companies to align social projects directly with business objectives to intentionally work toward eradicating poverty.   Humanity as a Scalable Asset: The future belongs to organizations that use technology to scale efficiency, but preserve humanity to scale innovation.   Performance vs. Pressure: True high-performance culture in the world of innovation is rooted in Psychological Safety, not pressure.   Lessons in AI Implementation: -Never automate without human oversight (unsupervised AI creates bias). -Leadership is the gatekeeper of culture—engagement must be top-down. -Take a gradual approach to allow for course corrections and ensure legal/operational security.   The Evolving HR Skillset: We live in an era where "busy-ness" is often mistaken for efficiency—where the time for reflection is disappearing and the boundaries between HR, Business, Tech, and Finance are blurring. To thrive, HR must: -Encourage the organization to act with intentionality. -Develop deep business acumen. -Design cultures strictly linked to strategic goals. -Lead actively with enough technical knowledge to accelerate execution.   The "Humanity Premium": Humanized leadership is the new competitive advantage. Genuinely caring for people is both cost-effective and highly impactful. In this era, managing burnout/fatigue and monitoring AI bias during implementation is mandatory.   The 4 C’s of Culture: An effective culture cannot be outsourced; it is the legacy of leadership. To implement it effectively, leaders must lead with: Clarity, Coherence, Consistency, and Courage. [Gustavo Anselmo](https://br.linkedin.com/in/gustavoanselmo/en?trk=public_post-text) Marcos Angelo [Rafael Barreto](https://br.linkedin.com/in/rafaelcbarreto?trk=public_post-text) [Cintia Salmazo (Ela/Ella/She)](https://br.linkedin.com/in/cintia-salmazo?trk=public_post-text) [Guilherme Corrêa](https://br.linkedin.com/in/guilhermercorrea?trk=public_post-text) Lais Marangoni [Enzo Fernandes](https://br.linkedin.com/in/enzo-fernandes?trk=public_post-text) [João Fernandes](https://br.linkedin.com/in/joao-fernandes04?trk=public_post-text) [Rebeca Bezerra](https://br.linkedin.com/in/rebeca-bezerra-3941b65b?trk=public_post-text) Isabelle Toledo Delfino [#HRSummit](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrsummit\&trk=public_post-text) [#Innovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finnovation\&trk=public_post-text) [#FutureOfWork](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofwork\&trk=public_post-text) [#Leadership](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fleadership\&trk=public_post-text) [#HybridIntelligence](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhybridintelligence\&trk=public_post-text) [#HumanResources](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhumanresources\&trk=public_post-text) [#StartSe](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstartse\&trk=public_post-text)

[76](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions) [8 Comments](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti\&trk=public_post_social-actions-comments)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti\&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ffernanda-lavini-ramos_hrsummit-innovation-futureofwork-activity-7444509836119195649-yAti\&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ninawaring1997_gartner-reveals-the-top-hr-trends-set-activity-7441966513265410048-i48y)

[](https://au.linkedin.com/in/ninawaring1997?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Nina Waring](https://au.linkedin.com/in/ninawaring1997?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fninawaring1997_gartner-reveals-the-top-hr-trends-set-activity-7441966513265410048-i48y\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

🚀 Gartner Reveals the Top HR Trends Set to Shape Australia’s Workforce in 2026 At the Gartner HR Symposium in Sydney, analysts unpacked the big forces reshaping how organisations will lead, transform and support their people next year: 2026 will be a pivotal year for HR. Here are the four trends every HR leader should have on their radar: 1️⃣ AI Is Redefining HR - FAST - AI adoption has exploded, with Gartner predicting that half of today’s HR tasks will be automated or AI‑supported by 2030. - HR functions are already feeling the shift, as CEOs, CFOs and CHROs all ask how AI will change HR’s role, cost base and strategic impact. 💡 The takeaway: HR needs its own AI strategy, one that reimagines work, processes and skills, not just tech adoption. 2️⃣ AI Is Becoming a Real Alternative to Talent In the past year alone: - 27% of organisations redesigned roles or skills because of AI - 24% redeployed employees due to tech‑driven redundancy - 10% have already replaced some roles with automation - This shift is pushing leaders to rethink how work gets done — and what work must remain human. 💡 The takeaway: CHROs need a “now‑next” talent plan that strengthens today’s workforce while preparing for tomorrow’s AI‑integrated roles. 3️⃣ The Growth vs. Efficiency Tightrope Gets Tighter - Leaders are under pressure to deliver growth and cut costs simultaneously. Yet 64% of CHROs say their leaders lack the mindset to lead change effectively. 💡 The takeaway: Change must be treated as an ongoing business discipline. HR’s role is to build leaders who can guide teams through constant transformation. 4️⃣ The Employment Deal Is Shifting — And Employees Feel It - Gartner reports a clear trend: organisations are expecting more from employees while offering less — more hours, more change, heavier workloads, but reduced flexibility and investment in culture. - Only 47% of CHROs believe their culture actually drives performance. 💡 The takeaway: CHROs must reshape the employee value proposition so it still attracts and retains talent in a high‑demand, low‑support environment. ✨ The Big Picture 2026 offers HR leaders both challenge and opportunity: ➡️ Lead AI transformation ➡️ Redesign work and talent strategies ➡️ Build resilient leaders ➡️ Rebuild the employee value proposition As Gartner’s Aaron McEwan reminded us, this is a year to reset priorities and equip teams for what’s next.

[7](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fninawaring1997_gartner-reveals-the-top-hr-trends-set-activity-7441966513265410048-i48y\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fninawaring1997_gartner-reveals-the-top-hr-trends-set-activity-7441966513265410048-i48y\&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fninawaring1997_gartner-reveals-the-top-hr-trends-set-activity-7441966513265410048-i48y\&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/blueprinthrco_spicy-fractional-truth-founder-edition-activity-7445458804399378432-FnMf)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/company/blueprinthrco?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[blueprintHR - fractional HR leadership](https://www.linkedin.com/company/blueprinthrco?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

56 followers

2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fblueprinthrco_spicy-fractional-truth-founder-edition-activity-7445458804399378432-FnMf\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

🌶️ Spicy Fractional Truth — Founder Edition #4 📈 If you're hiring past ~30 employees without HR infrastructure, it gets messy fast. Not immediately. At first, things still feel manageable. The founder knows everyone. Managers handle issues informally. Hiring is scrappy but it works. Then the company grows. 20 employees becomes 30. 30 becomes 40. And shit breaks. A few things might start happening all at once: 📊 Hiring speeds up 🧑💼 Managers now have real teams to lead 🤔 Employees start asking about career paths and compensation 📍 The company expands into new states or markets >> The systems that worked at 12 employees… start breaking at 35. >> Managers handle situations differently. >> Hiring processes vary by team. >> Performance conversations happen inconsistently. And CEOs start hearing things like: “Wait… how are we supposed to handle this situation?” This is the stage where companies usually realize something important: Culture doesn’t scale by accident. Neither do people systems. Growing companies need infrastructure like: ✔️ clear hiring processes ✔️ structured onboarding ✔️ performance management frameworks ✔️ compensation strategy and leveling ✔️ guidance for managers leading teams for the first time But hiring a $200K+ full-time HR executive still might not make sense yet. That’s exactly where fractional HR leadership can be incredibly effective. Not HR administration, but experienced HR leadership helping companies install the systems that support growth. Because once companies hit that 30–50 employee range, things either become: 🚀 intentionally scalable - OR - 🧯 permanently reactive. Founder Gut Check: 💬 At what company size did things start feeling more complicated than scrappy?

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fblueprinthrco_spicy-fractional-truth-founder-edition-activity-7445458804399378432-FnMf\&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fblueprinthrco_spicy-fractional-truth-founder-edition-activity-7445458804399378432-FnMf\&trk=public_post_comment-cta)

🌶️ Spicy Fractional Truth — Founder Edition #4 📈 If you're hiring past ~30 employees without HR infrastructure, it gets messy fast. Not immediately. At first, things still feel manageable. The founder knows everyone. Managers handle issues informally. Hiring is scrappy but it works. Then the company grows. 20 employees becomes 30. 30 becomes 40. And shit breaks. [](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/lilia-sanchez-avila_futureofhr-aiinhr-peopleandculture-activity-7446235141145874432-RvI2)

[](https://mx.linkedin.com/in/lilia-sanchez-avila?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Lilia Sánchez](https://mx.linkedin.com/in/lilia-sanchez-avila?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Flilia-sanchez-avila_futureofhr-aiinhr-peopleandculture-activity-7446235141145874432-RvI2\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

The role of HR in 2026 isn't just about adopting tools; it's about redesigning work from the ground up. I am incredibly passionate about how this shift empowers HR to be the driver of organizational intelligence, rather than just an administrative function. The goal is to move from experimentation to true, high-impact AI integration. [#FutureOfHR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffutureofhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#AIinHR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faiinhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#PeopleAndCulture](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpeopleandculture\&trk=public_post-text) [#HRTech2026](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhrtech2026\&trk=public_post-text) [#HuggingFace](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhuggingface\&trk=public_post-text)

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/successinhr?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Alan Collins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/successinhr?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

Best-selling Author of “The New HR Leader’s First 100 Days.” Former VP HR at PepsiCo. For more proven ways to accelerate your HR success, go to: SuccessInHR.com.

2mo

📌 HR LEADERS - When I first saw AI creeping into HR... This is what I immediately thought about... I thought back to the day Quaker Oats automated payroll. This happened decades ago & half our HR team freaked out!! The fear was clear: “If tech can do this, what do they need me for??” That same fear is everywhere again with AI. But here’s the truth: AI can do a lot of things. But it can’t do the MOST IMPORTANT things in HR. Here are 10 reminders for us as HR Leaders in the age of AI: 1. AI can scan resumes. 👉 It can’t spot potential. That's still your job. 2. AI can draft policies. 👉 It can’t win hearts. That's still your job. 3. AI can analyze turnover. 👉 It can’t coach a struggling manager. That's still your job. 4. AI can predict trends. 👉 It can’t build culture. This isn't solely you, but HR's role is crucial. 5. AI can suggest answers. 👉 It can’t have tough conversations. That's still your job. 6. AI can process feedback. 👉 It can’t show empathy. You can. 7. AI can flag risks. 👉It can’t build trust across the table. You can. 8. AI can map skills and competencies. 👉 It can’t inspire people to grow. You can. 9. AI can automate onboarding. 👉 It can’t make employees feel welcome. You & your leaders can. 10. AI can crunch HR metrics. 👉 It can’t lead change with courage. You & your leaders can. HERE'S THE BOTTOM LINE: ➡️ AI is here to stay. ➡️ But the value of HR has never been clearer ... we bring the humanity that AI can’t. ➡️ So don’t compete with AI—partner with it. ➡️ Let the tech handle the tasks. ➡️ You handle the trust, courage, and culture. That's my take. What's yours? Which of these 10 reminders resonates with you the most? -- 📌 P.S. HR LEADERS - Want more bold truths, personal stories, and actionable steps for staying on top of your game in HR? The check out: "STAY INSPIRED IN HR" at the link in the first comment below. This is your go-to guide for reigniting passion, purpose, and performance in your HR career. Whether you're navigating burnout, stuck in a toxic culture, or just craving a deeper sense of impact, this book delivers the motivation and mindset shifts you need. Again, check it out in the first comment below.

[6](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Flilia-sanchez-avila_futureofhr-aiinhr-peopleandculture-activity-7446235141145874432-RvI2\&trk=public_post_social-actions-reactions)

📌 HR LEADERS - When I first saw AI creeping into HR... This is what I immediately thought about... I thought back to the day Quaker Oats automated payroll. This happened decades ago & half our HR team freaked out!! The fear was clear: “If tech can do this, what do they need me for??” That same fear is everywhere again with AI. But here’s the truth: AI can do a lot of things. But it can’t do the MOST IMPORTANT things in HR. Here are 10 reminders for us as HR Leaders in the age of AI: 1. AI can scan resumes. 👉 It can’t spot potential. That's still your job. 2. AI can draft policies. 👉 It can’t win hearts. That's still your job. 3. AI can analyze turnover. 👉 It can’t coach a struggling manager. That's still your job. 4. AI can predict trends. 👉 It can’t build culture. This isn't solely you, but HR's role is crucial. 5. AI can suggest answers. 👉 It can’t have tough conversations. That's still your job. 6. AI can process feedback. 👉 It can’t show empathy. You can. 7. AI can flag risks. 👉It can’t build trust across the table. You can. 8. AI can map skills and competencies. 👉 It can’t inspire people to grow. You can. 9. AI can automate onboarding. 👉 It can’t make employees feel welcome. You & your leaders can. 10. AI can crunch HR metrics. 👉 It can’t lead change with courage. You & your leaders can. HERE'S THE BOTTOM LINE: ➡️ AI is here to stay. ➡️ But the value of HR has never been clearer ... we bring the humanity that AI can’t. ➡️ So don’t compete with AI—partner with it. ➡️ Let the tech handle the tasks. ➡️ You handle the trust, courage, and culture. 2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fchanda-lukonde-31457a131_great-insight-on-ai-and-hr-tech-indeed-activity-7445339356464119808-T0pK\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Great insight on AI and HR. Tech indeed is there to improve efficiency across industry. Advancement in AI technology can't be underscored, but rather embraced as an important too in enhancing the work flow, improve productivity and lessen occupational stress. What this means for HR, as I have noted in the write up and comments, AI will be more than a V- lookup, but a required assistant in all aspects of the HR roles. Because it's an assistant TOOL, it means, the Human aspect of HR completes the Equation in process formulation, policy and standard operating procedures, Legal frameworks and labour laws, culture development and more so, Empathy, fairness and negotiations.

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/successinhr?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

571 followers

2mo

[Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcitecommunity_hr-business-hr-activity-7444415541479391232-G-2-\&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect\&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

The HR Thought Leader: YouTube - Deloitte Insights on The Human Advantage in the Age of AI: l7kXEtca41U Context heading The drumbeat of AI grows louder. But while many focus on its disruptive power, Deloitte Insights pivots to a crucial question: where does the human advantage lie? This video delves into how C-suite leaders are grappling with workforce strategy in an AI-driven world, emphasizing the unique competitive edge that emerges when humans and AI work together. It's not about replacing; it's about augmenting. Why HR Leaders Should Watch This As HR leaders, we sit at the intersection of people and strategy. This isn't just a tech trend; it's a fundamental shift in how work gets done and how organizations compete. Understanding C-suite priorities around AI and workforce strategy is paramount. It helps us align our HR initiatives with top-level business goals. More importantly, it empowers us to champion the human element, ensuring our organizations harness AI to amplify human capabilities, not diminish them. This video could be a roadmap for proving HR's strategic value in the age of AI. 5 Key Takeaways 1. \*\*AI demands C-suite attention on workforce strategy.\*\* It's no longer just an IT or operations concern; it's a core strategic priority for the entire executive leadership. 2. \*\*The "human advantage" is real.\*\* This isn't just about AI's capabilities, but about the unique strengths humans bring when collaborating with AI. 3. \*\*Competitive advantage comes from collaboration.\*\* Organizations that effectively integrate human skills with AI tools will outperform those that don't. 4. \*\*HR must help define this new collaboration.\*\* We're instrumental in designing roles, training, and cultures that foster effective human-AI partnerships. 5. \*\*Deloitte Insights provides a strategic perspective.\*\* Their research often signals future trends, making this a forward-looking view for HR professionals. The HR Action Plan Your first step? Start making the case for human-AI collaboration as a competitive differentiator. Shift the narrative from "AI replacing jobs" to "AI enhancing human potential." Second, engage with your C-suite. Understand their specific concerns and priorities regarding AI and the workforce. Then, identify pilot programs where human-AI teams can demonstrate clear value. This could be in areas like data analysis, customer service, or creative problem-solving. Finally, focus on upskilling and reskilling your workforce. Equip your people with the critical thinking, creativity, and emotional intelligence that AI can't replicate, positioning them to thrive alongside intelligent systems. [#HR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#Business](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbusiness\&trk=public_post-text) [#hr](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhr\&trk=public_post-text) [#future](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffuture\&trk=public_post-text) [#people](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpeople\&trk=public_post-text) [#youtube](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fyoutube\&trk=public_post-text) [#shrm](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fshrm\&trk=public_post-text) [http://dlvr.it/TRn5GT](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fdlvr%2Eit%2FTRn5GT\&urlhash=lpN7\&trk=public_post-text) [#hr\_transformation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2FhrAbRtransformation\&trk=public_post-text) [#future\_of\_work](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2FfutureAbRofAbRwork\&trk=public_post-text) [#people\_analytics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2FpeopleAbRanalytics\&trk=public_post-text)

[Like](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcitecommunity_hr-business-hr-activity-7444415541479391232-G-2-\&trk=public_post_like-cta) [Comment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcitecommunity_hr-business-hr-activity-7444415541479391232-G-2-\&trk=public_post_comment-cta)
